title: Fix: Authorization Header Not Attached on CRUD Requests

requirements.text: Store the authentication token in localStorage (persistent storage) after successful login, so that it survives page refreshes and is not lost when held only in memory.

quotes: Store token in persistent storage (localStorage or equivalent).
Do NOT store token in memory only.

acceptanceCriteria: After a successful login, the token is written to localStorage under a consistent key.
After a page refresh, the token is still retrievable from localStorage.
The token is not solely kept in React state or a module-level variable.

requirements.text: Create a global HTTP request interceptor (or a shared API client utility) that automatically reads the token from localStorage and attaches the 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header to every POST, PUT, and DELETE request made to protected backend routes.

quotes: Create a global request interceptor: Automatically attach Authorization: Bearer <token>
Apply to all protected routes (POST, PUT, DELETE).
Token is read from storage on every request.

acceptanceCriteria: A centralized request utility or interceptor is created (e.g., frontend/src/utils/apiClient.ts).

All POST, PUT, and DELETE calls made from hooks (useAdminUsers, useVehicles, usePromotions, useTestimonials, useBlogPosts, useMediaAssets, useCreditSimulations, useContacts, useWebsiteSettings) use this interceptor.
Each outgoing protected request includes the 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header.
The token value is read from localStorage at the time each request is made (not cached in memory).
No 401 Unauthorized responses occur during normal authenticated usage.
The 'Session expired' error is no longer shown when saving data.

requirements.text: Ensure that on logout or token expiry, the token is removed from localStorage so that subsequent requests do not send a stale or invalid token.
acceptanceCriteria: When the user logs out, the token is cleared from localStorage.
If the token is expired or invalid, the user is redirected to login rather than silently failing with 401.

constraints: Do NOT modify any UI components or visual layout.
Do NOT modify the backend database schema, stable storage, or any Motoko backend logic.
Focus exclusively on frontend authentication token storage and request header attachment.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, and components/ui.
nonGoals: Changing the authentication provider (Internet Identity remains the sole auth provider).
Implementing token refresh logic beyond reading from localStorage.
